title: A MODIFIED CTAB METHOD FOR DNA EXTRACTION FROM OILY PLANTS OF BRASSICACEAE FAMILY
author: Maida Aslam, Amer Jamil, Fatma Hussain, Nisar Ahmed
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Extraction of DNA from secondary metabolite enriched medicinal plants has always been tricky. Numerous methods for DNA extraction have been presented but those either gives poor yield or employed to least chemical heterogenetic plants species with use of costly and hazardous chemicals and conditions that become worse for oily plants. To our knowledge only a few protocols have been reported for DNA extraction from oily plants that not only are laborious but also involve very complex reagents. Here we report simple DNA extraction strategy comparable to costly commercial DNA extraction kits, which is equally potent for oily and non-oily plants. High-molecular weight DNA was extracted without the use of liquid nitrogen and RNase A treatment that markedly reduce the extraction cost. Quantity of the DNA extracted from species that have lower nuclear DNA content was in the range of 250-380 µg/mL with A 260 /A 280  absorbance ratio 1.67-1.87 that confirmed purity of the recollected DNA samples. Quality of the extracted DNA was also evaluated by restriction analysis and PCR amplification. This protocol also extracts DNA efficiently from plant species belong to fabaceae and asteraceae families. Extracted DNA was suitable for molecular biology techniques sensitive to contaminants. 

  KEYWORDS:   Nuclear DNA, Oily plants, Brassicacea family, A 260 /A 280  absorbance, RNaseA
